_raw_content: You were never off your frequency. You were just gripping something that wasn't yours. Every belief you inherited, every role you performed to stay safe — those were static layered over a signal that never stopped broadcasting. The moment you stop reaching for who you should be, the body exhales into who you already are. Recalibration isn't addition. It's the courage to subtract.
slide_1_hook: You don't find your frequency — you stop abandoning it.
slide_2: Your nervous system has been broadcasting the real signal your entire life. You just couldn't hear it over the noise of everything you were told to be.
slide_3: You are allowed to stop performing the version of you that kept you safe at seven. That contract has expired. Your body knows.
slide_4: The shift isn't becoming someone new. It's the quiet devastation of realizing you left yourself a long time ago — and the exhale of walking back home.
slide_5: Feel your feet. Feel the weight of your body in this moment. This is the frequency. Not somewhere above you. Not earned. Here.
slide_6: I am not searching for myself. I am no longer leaving.
caption:
You were never lost.

You were just loud with someone else's instructions.

Every pattern you picked up — the people-pleasing, the hypervigilance, the chronic becoming — that was static. Survival software running on hardware that outgrew it years ago.

Your real frequency never left. It's underneath the performance. Underneath the proving. Underneath the tension your jaw holds when you think no one is watching.

Recalibration is not a strategy. It's a subtraction. You stop gripping. The body does the rest.

It will feel like loss at first. That's the old identity dissolving. Let it.

What's underneath has been waiting your whole life.
